Decompose waiver score into talent + matchup-fit components

Splits the single overall_score into two orthogonal axes:

  talent_score — sum of unweighted z-scores across applicable cats.
                 Matchup-blind value-above-replacement.
  fit_score    — sum of weighted z-scores restricted to flippable /
                 toss-up cats. Matchup-aware contribution.

The displayed overall_score is now an α-blend of the two:

  overall_score = α × talent_score + \(1 − α\) × fit_score

α decays linearly from 0.55 at season start to 0.10 at season end. Early
in the year category statuses come from tiny samples and are unreliable,
so we lean on raw talent. Late in the year every move matters for
standings, so fit dominates. The pipeline derives season_progress from
fantasy week / 24.

The legacy weighted_score is still exposed for transparency and a
single-player score_free_agent call still returns the legacy
overall_score == weighted_score behavior so existing callers see no
contract change.

EOF

## Summary
- Replaces the naive `actuals + per_game × days` projection math with Bayesian shrinkage for the four rate-driving projection columns: `proj_hr`, `proj_k`, `proj_walks_allowed`, and `proj_hits_allowed`.
- New `_shrink_projection_rates` helper in `matchup_analyzer.py` pulls each player's per-game projection rate toward their Statcast-implied prior, weighted by accumulated PA \(hitters\) or BF \(pitchers\).
- `project_week_totals` now accepts an optional `advanced_df` and, when supplied, runs the projections through the shrinkage step before merging with actuals. `daily_run._step_run_analysis` pre-fetches advanced stats for the union of both rosters and passes them into both `project_week_totals` calls.

## Priors used
- **HR/PA** ← `Barrel% × 0.006` \(stabilization K=170 PA\)
- **K/BF** ← `\(K-BB%\)/100 + 0.08` \(K=70 BF\)
- **WHIP** ← `5.7 × xwOBA-against − 0.50` \(K=770 BF\), shrunk in per-BF space, then split back proportionally between walks and hits to preserve each pitcher's BB/H ratio.

## Why
Early-season projections driven purely by season-to-date pace are extremely noisy: a hitter with 2 HR in 3 games projects 0.67 HR/game indefinitely. Shrinking toward a Statcast-derived prior regresses wild paces back to a defensible baseline while still rewarding players whose rolling results are consistent with their underlying metrics.

This is Step 4 of the 4-step statistical-model-quality plan. Stacked on #74 \(talent/fit α-blend\).

## Test plan
- [x] 6 new unit tests for `_shrink_projection_rates` covering: none/empty advanced_df pass-through, hot-HR regression toward Barrel prior, low-K pulled up toward K-BB prior, walks/hits joint shrinkage + proportional split, missing-player pass-through, zero-PA pass-through.
- [x] All existing 382 tests still pass \(388 total\).
- [x] `ruff format`, `ruff check`, `mypy` clean.

EOF

## Summary
Every stat on the roster tab \(hitters and pitchers\) is now colour-coded using the thresholds already documented in each column's tooltip. Added a compact legend above each table and grouped the columns under ``Weekly`` / ``Seasonal`` header bands so it's obvious which stats reset each fantasy week and which are season-to-date.

### Tier thresholds \(pulled from existing tooltips\)
| Stat | Elite | Great | Average | Poor |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| AVG | ≥ .300 | .270–.299 | .240–.269 | < .240 |
| OPS | ≥ .900 | .800–.899 | .700–.799 | < .700 |
| wOBA | ≥ .400 | .360–.399 | .320–.359 | < .320 |
| xwOBA | ≥ .380 | .340–.379 | .310–.339 | < .310 |
| Barrel% | ≥ 12 | 8–11 | 5–7 | < 5 |
| HardHit% | ≥ 50 | 42–49 | 35–41 | < 35 |
| SwSp% | ≥ 38 | 34–37 | 30–33 | < 30 |
| BatSp | ≥ 90 | 70–89 | 40–69 | < 40 |
| WHIP | < 1.00 | 1.00–1.20 | 1.21–1.35 | > 1.35 |
| K/BB | ≥ 5.0 | 3.5–4.9 | 2.5–3.4 | < 2.5 |
| xERA | < 3.00 | 3.00–3.75 | 3.76–4.50 | > 4.50 |
| xwOBA-A | < .290 | .290–.310 | .311–.330 | > .330 |
| K-BB% | ≥ 20 | 15–19 | 10–14 | < 10 |
| Brl%-A | < 5 | 5–7 | 8–10 | > 10 |

Counting stats \(H, HR, SB, BB, W, K, SV+H\) have only 3 documented buckets and so skip the Elite tier. Launch Angle is a range-type stat: power-zone 15–22° is ``great``, outer sweet-spot 8–32° is ``average``, else ``poor``.

### Weekly vs Seasonal header grouping
- **Hitters — Weekly** \(week-to-date from ``fact_player_stats_daily``\): H, HR, SB, BB, AVG, OPS
- **Hitters — Seasonal** \(from ``fact_player_advanced_stats``\): wOBA, xwOBA, Barrel%, HardHit%, LA, SwSp%, BatSp
- **Pitchers — Weekly**: W, K, WHIP, K/BB, SV+H
- **Pitchers — Seasonal**: xERA, xwOBA-A, K-BB%, Brl%-A

### Implementation
- New ``_STAT_TIER_FNS`` maps each stat key to a tier function.
- ``_tier_for`` → ``_color_tier`` → ``_fmt_stat_tier`` / ``_fmt_adv_tier`` wrap the existing formatters without changing their signatures.
- ``_html_table`` gained an optional ``group_headers`` kwarg: a list of ``\(label, colspan\)`` tuples rendered as a second header row above the column headers.
- ``hitter_roster_ui`` and ``pitcher_roster_ui`` now prepend ``_roster_tier_legend\(\)`` and pass ``group_headers``.
- The old ad-hoc WHIP coloring in the pitcher table was replaced with the tier system \(its previous thresholds matched what the tier function does anyway\).

## Test plan
- [x] 11 new unit tests: tier resolution for higher-is-better / lower-is-better / 3-tier / range-type / NaN / unknown; ``_color_tier`` wrapping and dash pass-through; legend rendering; ``_html_table`` group-header rendering.
- [x] 408 tests pass \(up from 397\), 84.25% coverage.
- [x] ``ruff format``, ``ruff check``, ``mypy`` clean.

EOF
